content.claim: Hungary and the EU agreed a permanent teacher pay-rise programme worth €1.8 billion (HUF 735 billion) for 2024–2030/31, raising average pay to 80% of the average graduate wage, with EU conditionality locked to 31 December 2030.
content.context: Rural school consolidation in Hungary is durably feasible through 2030 if anchored to EU pay conditionality and church-led preservation, but carries high reversal risk because maintainer status is politically contingent, cross-party institutional safeguards do not exist, and structural demographic pressure means reform cycles will recur.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
